name: CARDIAC CONDUCTION DEVICES (CCD
definition: electronic device providing small electrical stimuli for cardiac contraction as substitute for AUTOMATIC IMPLANTABLE CARDIOVERTER-DEFIBRILLATOR (AICD) electronic device generating a large amount of electrical energy to defibrillate the heart

demographics.Location: electrode tip in right atrial appendage

distribution: electrode tip in right atrial appendage
discriminating_features: electrode tip may be shaped into a preformed "J loop"
slight redundancy of lead desirable to avoid tension during deep inspiration / arm
electrode tip to left of spine (frontal view)

general: electrode tip may be shaped into a preformed "J loop"
slight redundancy of lead desirable to avoid tension during deep inspiration / arm
electrode tip to left of spine (frontal view)

clinical.Rx: cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT) in combination with implantable
